codeReview.summary: Reviewed the graphql-comments-query diff, handoff, transcript skeleton, and current server implementation. The feature correctly exposes issue(id).comments(first, orderBy: createdAt) with ascending chronological ordering, stable tie-breaking by id, ISO DateTime serialization, nested user resolution, and empty-node behavior covered by integration tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill's TDD procedure is too rigid for partially implemented features. This worker added missing coverage first and then tightened the implementation because the assigned comments query already existed; the skill should explicitly allow that path instead of requiring an artificial red phase.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md:31-37 requires a red phase, while the handoff for graphql-comments-query records followedProcedure=false and explains the feature was already partially implemented in /Users/chris/.factory/missions/6774ac71-5dc9-4936-9303-c902a364ac7c/handoffs/2026-04-01T14-40-19-941Z__graphql-comments-query__41c8e368-3d26-46b4-8402-bdf42eb4ce68.json:76-85

summary: Pass. I found no blocking or non-blocking correctness defects in this feature. The comments query implementation and tests match the feature contract; the only follow-up is a skill-documentation gap around handling partially pre-implemented work.
